html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Five — Part One

The Torah — Fifth Book

DEVARIM

Words

Chapters 1 through 17

The First and Second Addresses of Moshe — The History Retold — The Shema — The Covenant Law

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

1QDeuta (1Q4) · 1QDeutb (1Q5) · 4QDeuta (4Q28) · 4QDeutb (4Q29)

4QDeutc (4Q30) · 4QDeutd (4Q31) · 4QDeute (4Q32) · 4QDeutf (4Q33)

4QDeutg (4Q34) · 4QDeuth (4Q35) · 4QDeuti (4Q36) · 4QDeutj (4Q37)

4QDeutk1 (4Q38) · 4QDeutk2 (4Q38a) · 4QDeutl (4Q39) · 4QDeutm (4Q40)

4QDeutn (4Q41) · 4QDeuto (4Q42) · 4QDeutp (4Q43) · 4QDeutq (4Q44)

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Beta Israel Ancient Canon

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitutions

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

THE FIRST ADDRESS OF MOSHE — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 4

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 1 The Setting — The Retelling of the Wilderness Journey Begins

1:1 These are the words that Moshe spoke to all Yisra'EL beyond the Yarden in the wilderness, in the Aravah opposite Suf, between Paran and Tofel, Lavan, Hatzerot, and Di-Zahav.

1:2 It is eleven days' journey from Horev by the way of Mount Se'ir to Kadesh-Barnea.

1:3 In the fortieth year, on the first day of the eleventh month, Moshe spoke to the people of Yisra'EL according to all that YHWH had given him in commandment to them.

1:6 YHWH our Creator said to us in Horev: You have stayed long enough at this mountain.

1:7 Turn and take your journey, and go to the hill country of the Amorites and to all their neighbors in the Aravah, in the hill country and in the lowland and in the Negev and by the seacoast, the land of the Kena'ani, and Lebanon, as far as the great river, the river Perat.

1:8 See, I have set the land before you. Go in and take possession of the land that YHWH swore to your fathers, to Avraham, to Yitzhak, and to Yaakov, to give to them and to their offspring after them.

1:16 And I charged your judges at that time: Hear the cases between your brothers, and judge righteously between a man and his brother or the alien who is with him.

1:17 You shall not be partial in judgment. You shall hear the small and the great alike. You shall not be intimidated by anyone, for the judgment is The Creator's. And the case that is too hard for you, you shall bring to me, and I will hear it.

1:21 See, YHWH your Creator has set the land before you. Go up, take possession, as YHWH, The Creator of your fathers, has told you. Do not fear or be dismayed.

1:26 Yet you would not go up, but rebelled against the command of YHWH your Creator.

1:30 YHWH your Creator who goes before you will himself fight for you, just as he did for you in Egypt before your eyes,

1:31 And in the wilderness, where you have seen how YHWH your Creator carried you, as a man carries his son, all the way that you went until you came to this place.

1:35 Not one of these men of this evil generation shall see the good land that I swore to give to your fathers,

1:36 Except Kalev the son of Yefunneh. He shall see it, and to him and to his children I will give the land on which he trod, because he has wholly followed YHWH.

1:37 Even with me YHWH was angry on your account and said: You also shall not go in there.

1:38 Yehoshua the son of Nun, who stands before you, he shall enter. Encourage him, for he shall cause Yisra'EL to inherit it.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] Devarim is the most attested Torah book in the Qumran corpus — more manuscripts of Devarim were found at Qumran than any other book. Over thirty manuscripts containing portions of Devarim have been identified across multiple caves. 4QDeuta through 4QDeutq represent the main Cave 4 manuscripts. 1QDeuta and 1QDeutb come from Cave 1. The opening address of Moshe is attested in multiple Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 1 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1:31 — YHWH your Creator carried you, as a man carries his son, all the way that you went. This parental metaphor for YHWH's relationship to Yisra'EL in the wilderness appears in the midst of Moshe's recounting of the failure at Kadesh-Barnea. Even as he rehearses the rebellion, Moshe frames YHWH's wilderness care in the most intimate terms possible: a father carrying a son. The covenant relationship is not primarily legal — it is familial. The father carried the child even when the child rebelled. The covenant endures through failure because the father does not abandon the son.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 2 The Years of Wandering — Edom, Mo'av, Ammon — Victory over Sihon

2:1 Then we turned and journeyed into the wilderness in the direction of the Red Sea, as YHWH told me. And for many days we traveled around Mount Se'ir.

2:4 And command the people: You are about to pass through the territory of your brothers, the people of Esav, who live in Se'ir. And they will be afraid of you. So be very careful.

2:5 Do not contend with them, for I will not give you any of their land, no, not so much as for the sole of the foot to tread on, because I have given Mount Se'ir to Esav as a possession.

2:7 For YHWH your Creator has blessed you in all the work of your hands. He knows your going through this great wilderness. These forty years YHWH your Creator has been with you. You have lacked nothing.

2:9 And YHWH said to me: Do not harass Mo'av or contend with them in battle, for I will not give you any of their land for a possession, because I have given Ar to the people of Lot for a possession.

2:19 And when you approach the territory of the people of Ammon, do not harass them or contend with them, for I will not give you any of the land of the people of Ammon as a possession, because I have given it to the sons of Lot for a possession.

2:24 Rise up, set out on your journey and go over the valley of the Arnon. Behold, I have given into your hand Sihon the Amorite, king of Heshbon, and his land. Begin to take possession, and contend with him in battle.

2:30 But Sihon the king of Heshbon would not let us pass by him, for YHWH your Creator hardened his spirit and made his heart obstinate, that he might give him into your hand, as he is this day.

2:33 And YHWH our Creator gave him over to us, and we defeated him and his sons and all his people.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeuta and 4QDeutb preserve portions of Chapter 2.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 2:5-19 — YHWH explicitly restrains Yisra'EL from taking the territories of Edom, Mo'av, and Ammon — peoples descended from Esav and from Lot. The covenant land grant is precisely bounded. YHWH has assigned specific territories to other peoples. Yisra'EL's conquest is not a universal license to take any land — it is specific to the territory YHWH designated as the covenant inheritance. The ancient record is deliberate about what Yisra'EL is not permitted to take and why. YHWH honors his grants to other peoples while executing his covenant grant to Yisra'EL.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 3 Victory over Og — Moshe Views the Land — Yehoshua Commissioned

3:1 Then we turned and went up the way to Bashan. And Og the king of Bashan came out against us, he and all his people, to battle at Edre'i.

3:2 But YHWH said to me: Do not fear him, for I have given him and all his people and his land into your hand. And you shall do to him as you did to Sihon the king of the Amorites, who lived at Heshbon.

3:11 For only Og the king of Bashan was left of the remnant of the Refaim. Behold, his bed was a bed of iron. Is it not in Rabbah of the Ammonites? Nine cubits was its length and four cubits its breadth, according to the common cubit.

3:23 And I pleaded with YHWH at that time, saying:

3:24 O Lord YHWH, you have only begun to show your servant your greatness and your mighty hand. For what deity is there in heaven or on earth who can do such works and mighty acts as yours?

3:25 Please let me go over and see the good land beyond the Yarden, that good hill country and Lebanon.

3:26 But YHWH was angry with me because of you and would not listen to me. And YHWH said to me: Enough from you — do not speak to me of this matter again.

3:27 Go up to the top of Pisgah and lift up your eyes westward and northward and southward and eastward, and look at it with your eyes, for you shall not go over this Yarden.

3:28 But commission Yehoshua, and encourage and strengthen him, for he shall go over at the head of this people, and he shall put them in possession of the land that you shall see.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeuta and 4QDeutb preserve portions of Chapter 3.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 3:11 — Og the king of Bashan was left of the remnant of the Refaim. His iron bed was nine cubits long. The Refaim are a people described as giants in multiple passages of the Torah and the Nevi'im. The Chanokh literature — physically confirmed at Qumran — identifies the Refaim as connected to the Nephilim lineage, descendants of the Watcher unions described in Bereshit 6. Og's iron bed — more than four meters long by the common cubit — is cited as physical evidence of his size. The ancient record preserves the detail of the bed's dimensions, stored in Rabbah of the Ammonites, as a verifiable artifact.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 4 The Call to Obedience — The Uniqueness of YHWH — Cities of Refuge

4:1 And now, O Yisra'EL, listen to the statutes and the rules that I am teaching you, and do them, that you may live, and go in and take possession of the land that YHWH, The Creator of your fathers, is giving you.

4:2 You shall not add to the word that I command you, nor take from it, that you may keep the commandments of YHWH your Creator that I command you.

4:7 For what great nation is there that has a deity so near to it as YHWH our Creator is to us, whenever we call upon him?

4:8 And what great nation is there, that has statutes and rules so righteous as all this law that I set before you today?

4:9 Only take care, and keep your soul diligently, lest you forget the things that your eyes have seen, and lest they depart from your heart all the days of your life. Make them known to your children and your children's children.

4:10 How on the day that you stood before YHWH your Creator at Horev, YHWH said to me: Gather the people to me, that I may let them hear my words, so that they may learn to fear me all the days that they live on the earth, and that they may teach their children so.

4:12 Then YHWH spoke to you out of the midst of the fire. You heard the sound of words, but saw no form — there was only a voice.

4:15 Therefore watch yourselves very carefully. Since you saw no form on the day that YHWH spoke to you at Horev out of the midst of the fire,

4:16 Beware lest you act corruptly by making a carved image for yourselves, in the form of any figure, the likeness of male or female.

4:23 Take care, lest you forget the covenant of YHWH your Creator, which he made with you, and make a carved image, the form of anything that YHWH your Creator has forbidden you.

4:29 But from there you will seek YHWH your Creator and you will find him, if you search after him with all your heart and with all your soul.

4:30 When you are in tribulation, and all these things come upon you in the latter days, you will return to YHWH your Creator and obey his voice.

4:31 For YHWH your Creator is a merciful Creator. He will not leave you or destroy you or forget the covenant with your fathers that he swore to them.

4:32 For ask now of the days that are past, which were before you, since the day that The Creator created man on the earth, and ask from one end of heaven to the other, whether such a great thing as this has ever happened or was ever heard of.

4:35 To you it was shown, that you might know that YHWH is The Creator — there is no other besides him.

4:39 Know therefore today, and lay it to your heart, that YHWH is The Creator in heaven above and on the earth beneath — there is no other.

4:40 Therefore you shall keep his statutes and his commandments, which I command you today, that it may go well with you and with your children after you, and that you may prolong your days in the land that YHWH your Creator is giving you for all time.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeuta, 4QDeutb, and 4QDeutc preserve portions of Chapter 4. The declaration of verse 4:35 — YHWH is The Creator, there is no other besides him — is among the most attested phrases in the Qumran Devarim manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 4 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 4:12 — you heard the sound of words, but saw no form — there was only a voice. The Sinai theophany is explicitly characterized as auditory, not visual. The people heard YHWH — they did not see him. Moshe draws the direct implication in verse 4:15-16: because you saw no form, do not make any carved image. The prohibition of images is grounded in the nature of the Sinai encounter itself. YHWH chose to reveal himself through speech, not through visible form. Any image made to represent YHWH is therefore a falsification of the Sinai revelation — it attributes a visible form to the one who specifically refused visible form at the covenant's founding moment.

THE SECOND ADDRESS — THE SHEMA — THE COVENANT LAW — CHAPTERS 5 THROUGH 11

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 5 The Ten Words Restated — The Covenant at Horev

5:1 And Moshe summoned all Yisra'EL and said to them: Hear, O Yisra'EL, the statutes and the rules that I speak in your hearing today, and you shall learn them and be careful to do them.

5:2 YHWH our Creator made a covenant with us in Horev.

5:3 Not with our fathers did YHWH make this covenant, but with us, who are all of us here alive today.

5:6 I am YHWH your Creator, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.

5:7 You shall have no other deities before me.

5:8 You shall not make for yourself a carved image, or any likeness of anything that is in heaven above, or that is on the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth.

5:11 You shall not take the name of YHWH your Creator in vain, for YHWH will not hold him guiltless who takes his name in vain.

5:12 Observe the Sabbath day, to keep it holy, as YHWH your Creator commanded you.

5:13 Six days you shall labor and do all your work,

5:14 But the seventh day is a Sabbath to YHWH your Creator. On it you shall not do any work, you or your son or your daughter or your male servant or your female servant, or your ox or your donkey or any of your livestock, or the sojourner who is within your gates, that your male servant and your female servant may rest as well as you.

5:15 You shall remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt, and YHWH your Creator brought you out from there with a mighty hand and an outstretched arm. Therefore YHWH your Creator commanded you to keep the Sabbath day.

5:16 Honor your father and your mother, as YHWH your Creator commanded you, that your days may be long, and that it may go well with you in the land that YHWH your Creator is giving you.

5:17 You shall not murder.

5:18 And you shall not commit adultery.

5:19 And you shall not steal.

5:20 And you shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.

5:21 And you shall not covet your neighbor's wife. And you shall not desire your neighbor's house, his field, or his male servant, or his female servant, his ox, or his donkey, or anything that is your neighbor's.

5:22 These words YHWH spoke to all your assembly at the mountain out of the midst of the fire, the cloud, and the thick darkness, with a loud voice — and he added no more. And he wrote them on two tablets of stone and gave them to me.

5:29 Oh that they had such a heart as this always, to fear me and to keep all my commandments, that it might go well with them and with their descendants forever.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeuta, 4QDeutb, 4QDeutc, and 4QDeuto preserve portions of Chapter 5. The Ten Words as stated in Devarim show several variations from the Shemot 20 version — most notably the Sabbath grounding. In Shemot 20:11 the Sabbath is grounded in creation — YHWH rested on the seventh day. In Devarim 5:15 the Sabbath is grounded in the Exodus — you were a slave in Egypt. The DSS manuscripts preserve both groundings in their respective contexts without harmonizing them.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 5 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 5:22 — these words YHWH spoke to all your assembly at the mountain out of the midst of the fire — and he added no more. The Ten Words are distinguished from all other Torah legislation by this declaration: YHWH spoke these words directly to the entire assembly, not through Moshe as intermediary. And he added no more — the Ten Words are a complete and closed direct divine speech. All other covenant legislation comes through Moshe. The Ten Words come directly from YHWH to the people. This distinction is embedded in the ancient text of Devarim itself.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 6 The Shema — Love YHWH — The Command to Teach the Children

6:1 Now this is the commandment — the statutes and the rules — that YHWH your Creator commanded me to teach you, that you may do them in the land to which you are going over, to possess it,

6:2 That you may fear YHWH your Creator, you and your son and your son's son, by keeping all his statutes and his commandments, which I command you, all the days of your life, and that your days may be long.

6:3 Hear therefore, O Yisra'EL, and be careful to do them, that it may go well with you, and that you may multiply greatly, as YHWH, The Creator of your fathers, has promised you, in a land flowing with milk and honey.

6:4 Hear, O Yisra'EL: YHWH our Creator, YHWH is one.

6:5 You shall love YHWH your Creator with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your might.

6:6 And these words that I command you today shall be on your heart.

6:7 You shall teach them diligently to your children, and shall talk of them when you sit in your house, and when you walk by the way, and when you lie down, and when you rise.

6:8 You shall bind them as a sign on your hand, and they shall be as frontlets between your eyes.

6:9 You shall write them on the doorposts of your house and on your gates.

6:10 And when YHWH your Creator brings you into the land that he swore to your fathers, to Avraham, to Yitzhak, and to Yaakov, to give you — with great and good cities that you did not build,

6:12 Then take care lest you forget YHWH, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.

6:13 It is YHWH your Creator you shall fear. Him you shall serve and by his name you shall swear.

6:20 When your son asks you in time to come: What is the meaning of the testimonies and the statutes and the rules that YHWH our Creator has commanded you?

6:21 Then you shall say to your son: We were Pharaoh's slaves in Egypt. And YHWH brought us out of Egypt with a mighty hand.

6:24 And YHWH commanded us to do all these statutes, to fear YHWH our Creator, for our good always, that he might preserve us alive, as we are this day.

6:25 And it will be righteousness for us, if we are careful to do all this commandment before YHWH our Creator, as he has commanded us.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeuta, 4QDeutb, 4QDeuto, 4QDeutq, and multiple other Cave 4 manuscripts preserve portions of Chapter 6. The Shema — verse 6:4 — is the single most attested verse in the entire Qumran corpus. It is found in phylactery texts — tefillin — physically discovered at Qumran, confirming the ancient practice of binding these words as signs on the hand and forehead as commanded in verse 6:8. The Nash Papyrus — discovered in Egypt and dated to approximately 150-100 BCE — contains the Shema and the Ten Words, making it one of the oldest surviving Hebrew manuscript fragments outside the DSS.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 6 in full. The Ethiopian Beta Israel community recites the Shema as a central covenant declaration.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6:4 — Shema Yisra'EL YHWH Elohenu YHWH Ekhad. Hear O Yisra'EL — YHWH our Creator — YHWH is one. The Shema is the foundational declaration of the entire covenant theology. YHWH is one — ekhad — uniquely, incomparably one. The declaration is not primarily a statement about numerical singularity but about YHWH's absolute uniqueness and undivided sovereignty. The name YHWH appears twice in the four Hebrew words of the declaration — the covenant name bookending the confession of his oneness. This verse is physically attested in more DSS manuscripts than any other single verse in the Torah.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6:7 — you shall teach them diligently to your children, when you sit in your house, when you walk by the way, when you lie down, when you rise. The four life settings — sitting, walking, lying down, rising — constitute the entirety of daily life. The covenant teaching obligation has no off-hours. The ancient Hebrew curriculum is not a scheduled event — it is woven into every transition of the day. The covenant community teaches its children not in dedicated educational institutions alone but in the continuous movement of ordinary life.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 7 Separation from the Nations — The Covenant Faithfulness of YHWH

7:1 When YHWH your Creator brings you into the land that you are entering to take possession of it, and clears away many nations before you — the Hittites, the Girgashites, the Amorites, the Kena'anim, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Yevusim, seven nations more numerous and mightier than you —

7:2 And when YHWH your Creator gives them over to you, and you defeat them, then you must devote them to complete destruction. You shall make no covenant with them and show no mercy to them.

7:6 For you are a people holy to YHWH your Creator. YHWH your Creator has chosen you to be a people for his treasured possession, out of all the peoples who are on the face of the earth.

7:7 It was not because you were more in number than any other people that YHWH set his love on you and chose you, for you were the fewest of all peoples,

7:8 But it is because YHWH loves you and is keeping the oath that he swore to your fathers, that YHWH has brought you out with a mighty hand and redeemed you from the house of slavery, from the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt.

7:9 Know therefore that YHWH your Creator is The Creator, the faithful Creator who keeps covenant and steadfast love with those who love him and keep his commandments, to a thousand generations,

7:12 And because you listen to these rules and keep and do them, YHWH your Creator will keep with you the covenant and the steadfast love that he swore to your fathers.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutc and 4QDeutd preserve portions of Chapter 7.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 7 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7:7-8 — not because you were more in number — you were the fewest of all peoples — but because YHWH loves you and is keeping the oath he swore to your fathers. The selection of Yisra'EL as a covenant people is explicitly grounded not in their size, power, or virtue but in two things: YHWH's love and YHWH's faithfulness to his sworn oath to the patriarchs. The covenant election is not a reward for merit. It is an expression of YHWH's character — loving and faithful. The Avrahamic covenant oath is again the foundation. YHWH's love chose, and YHWH's faithfulness to his own oath maintains the choice.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 8 Remember YHWH in the Land — The Danger of Forgetting

8:2 And you shall remember the whole way that YHWH your Creator has led you these forty years in the wilderness, that he might humble you, testing you to know what was in your heart, whether you would keep his commandments or not.

8:3 And he humbled you and let you hunger and fed you with manna, which you did not know, nor did your fathers know, that he might make you know that man does not live by bread alone, but man lives by every word that comes from the mouth of YHWH.

8:4 Your clothing did not wear out on you and your foot did not swell these forty years.

8:5 Know then in your heart that, as a man disciplines his son, YHWH your Creator disciplines you.

8:7 For YHWH your Creator is bringing you into a good land, a land of brooks of water, of fountains and springs, flowing out in the valleys and hills,

8:10 And you shall eat and be full, and you shall bless YHWH your Creator for the good land he has given you.

8:11 Take care lest you forget YHWH your Creator by not keeping his commandments and his rules and his statutes, which I command you today,

8:14 Then your heart be lifted up, and you forget YHWH your Creator, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.

8:17 Beware lest you say in your heart: My power and the might of my hand have gotten me this wealth.

8:18 You shall remember YHWH your Creator, for it is he who gives you power to get wealth, that he may confirm his covenant that he swore to your fathers, as it is this day.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutc and 4QDeutd preserve portions of Chapter 8.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 8 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 8:3 — man does not live by bread alone, but man lives by every word that comes from the mouth of YHWH. The manna in the wilderness was not only physical provision — it was a theological lesson about the source of life. YHWH provided bread that had never existed before so that the people would know that life itself depends on YHWH's word, not on the reliability of ordinary food systems. The forty years of manna were a forty-year curriculum in radical dependence on YHWH's provision. The lesson is stated plainly in Moshe's address: the wilderness was designed to teach this.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 9 Not Because of Your Righteousness — The Golden Calf Recalled

9:1 Hear, O Yisra'EL: You are to cross over the Yarden today, to go in to dispossess nations greater and mightier than you, cities great and fortified up to heaven,

9:4 Do not say in your heart, after YHWH your Creator has thrust them out before you: It is because of my righteousness that YHWH has brought me in to possess this land.

9:5 Not because of your righteousness or the uprightness of your heart are you going in to possess their land, but because of the wickedness of these nations YHWH your Creator is driving them out from before you, and that he may confirm the word that YHWH swore to your fathers, to Avraham, to Yitzhak, and to Yaakov.

9:6 Know, therefore, that YHWH your Creator is not giving you this good land to possess because of your righteousness, for you are a stubborn people.

9:9 When I went up the mountain to receive the tablets of stone, the tablets of the covenant that YHWH made with you, I remained on the mountain forty days and forty nights. I neither ate bread nor drank water.

9:10 And YHWH gave me the two tablets of stone written with the finger of The Creator, and on them were all the words that YHWH had spoken with you on the mountain out of the midst of the fire on the day of the assembly.

9:18 Then I lay prostrate before YHWH as before, forty days and forty nights. I neither ate bread nor drank water, because of all the sin that you had committed, in doing what was evil in the sight of YHWH to provoke him to anger.

9:19 For I was afraid of the anger and hot displeasure that YHWH bore against you, so that he was ready to destroy you. But YHWH listened to me that time also.

9:26 And I prayed to YHWH: O Lord YHWH, do not destroy your people and your heritage, whom you have redeemed through your greatness, whom you have brought out of Egypt with a mighty hand.

9:27 Remember your servants, Avraham, Yitzhak, and Yaakov. Do not regard the stubbornness of this people, or their wickedness, or their sin.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutd and 4QDeute preserve portions of Chapter 9.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 9 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9:4-6 — not because of your righteousness. Three times in three verses Moshe states that Yisra'EL's covenant inheritance of the land is not grounded in their moral merit. The land transfer happens because of the wickedness of the prior inhabitants and because of YHWH's covenant oath to the patriarchs. The ancient record is ruthlessly honest about the grounds of covenant blessing: it is YHWH's character and YHWH's faithfulness to his own word, not human virtue. The covenant people are explicitly called stubborn — verse 9:6. The blessing is not their reward. It is YHWH's keeping of his own promise.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 10 The Second Tablets — The Ark — The Summary of YHWH's Requirements

10:1 At that time YHWH said to me: Cut for yourself two tablets of stone like the first, and come up to me on the mountain and make an ark of wood.

10:4 And he wrote on the tablets, in the same writing as before, the Ten Words that YHWH had spoken to you on the mountain out of the midst of the fire on the day of the assembly. And YHWH gave them to me.

10:5 Then I turned and came down from the mountain and put the tablets in the ark that I had made. And there they are, as YHWH commanded me.

10:12 And now, Yisra'EL, what does YHWH your Creator require of you, but to fear YHWH your Creator, to walk in all his ways, to love him, to serve YHWH your Creator with all your heart and with all your soul,

10:13 And to keep the commandments and statutes of YHWH, which I am commanding you today for your good?

10:14 Behold, to YHWH your Creator belong heaven and the heaven of heavens, the earth with all that is in it.

10:15 Yet YHWH set his heart in love on your fathers and chose their offspring after them, you above all peoples, as you are this day.

10:16 Circumcise therefore the foreskin of your heart, and be no longer stubborn.

10:17 For YHWH your Creator is The Creator of deities and Lord of lords, the great, the mighty, and the awesome Creator, who is not partial and takes no bribe.

10:18 He executes justice for the fatherless and the widow, and loves the sojourner, giving him food and clothing.

10:19 Love the sojourner, therefore, for you were sojourners in the land of Egypt.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeute and 4QDeutf preserve portions of Chapter 10.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 10 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 10:12-13 — what does YHWH your Creator require of you, but to fear YHWH, to walk in all his ways, to love him, to serve him with all your heart and with all your soul, and to keep the commandments for your good? The summary of the covenant obligation in four verbs: fear, walk, love, serve. The Shema commands love of YHWH with all your heart, soul, and might — Devarim 6:5. Chapter 10 adds the structural framework: fear produces walking in his ways, love produces service, and service expresses itself in keeping the commandments. The entire covenant law hangs on this relational structure.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 10:17-19 — YHWH executes justice for the fatherless and the widow and loves the sojourner, giving him food and clothing. Love the sojourner therefore, for you were sojourners in Egypt. The covenant ethics of Chapter 19 in Vayikra — love the stranger as yourself — are grounded here in YHWH's own character and action. YHWH loves sojourners. YHWH feeds and clothes them. Therefore the covenant people who were themselves sojourners in Egypt are to mirror YHWH's character in their treatment of the foreigner among them.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 11 The Land — The Blessing and the Curse — Gerizim and Eval

11:1 You shall therefore love YHWH your Creator and keep his charge, his statutes, his rules, and his commandments always.

11:8 You shall therefore keep the whole commandment that I command you today, that you may be strong, and go in and take possession of the land that you are going over to possess,

11:10 For the land that you are entering to take possession of it is not like the land of Egypt, from which you have come, where you sowed your seed and irrigated it, like a garden of vegetables.

11:11 But the land that you are going over to possess is a land of hills and valleys, which drinks water by the rain from heaven,

11:12 A land that YHWH your Creator cares for. The eyes of YHWH your Creator are always upon it, from the beginning of the year to the end of the year.

11:13 And if you will indeed obey my commandments that I command you today, to love YHWH your Creator, and to serve him with all your heart and with all your soul,

11:14 He will give the rain for your land in its season, the early rain and the later rain, that you may gather in your grain and your wine and your oil.

11:18 You shall therefore lay up these words of mine in your heart and in your soul, and you shall bind them as a sign on your hand, and they shall be as frontlets between your eyes.

11:26 See, I am setting before you today a blessing and a curse:

11:27 The blessing, if you obey the commandments of YHWH your Creator, which I command you today,

11:28 And the curse, if you do not obey the commandments of YHWH your Creator, but turn aside from the way that I am commanding you today, to go after other deities that you have not known.

11:29 And when YHWH your Creator brings you into the land that you are entering to take possession of it, you shall set the blessing on Mount Gerizim and the curse on Mount Eval.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutf and 4QDeutg preserve portions of Chapter 11.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 11 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 11:12 — a land that YHWH your Creator cares for. The eyes of YHWH your Creator are always upon it, from the beginning of the year to the end of the year. The covenant land is described as under YHWH's continuous personal attention. Unlike Egypt, which depends on the Nile — a mechanical, predictable water source — the covenant land depends on rain from heaven. This makes it a land of continuous dependency on YHWH's provision. The agricultural cycle of the covenant land is a year-long covenant relationship: YHWH provides rain when the people keep covenant. The land and the covenant are inseparable in the ancient record.

THE COVENANT LEGISLATION — CHAPTERS 12 THROUGH 17

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 12 The One Place of Worship — The Centralization Command

12:1 These are the statutes and rules that you shall be careful to do in the land that YHWH, The Creator of your fathers, has given you to possess, all the days that you live on the earth.

12:2 You shall surely destroy all the places where the nations whom you shall dispossess served their deities, on the high mountains and on the hills and under every green tree.

12:4 You shall not worship YHWH your Creator in that way.

12:5 But you shall seek the place that YHWH your Creator will choose out of all your tribes to put his name and make his habitation there. There you shall go,

12:6 And there you shall bring your burnt offerings and your sacrifices, your tithes and the contribution that you present, your vow offerings, your freewill offerings, and the firstborn of your herd and of your flock.

12:11 Then to the place that YHWH your Creator will choose, to make his name dwell there, there you shall bring all that I command you: your burnt offerings and your sacrifices, your tithes and the contribution that you present, and all your finest vow offerings that you vow to YHWH.

12:13 Take care that you do not offer your burnt offerings at any place that you see,

12:14 But at the place that YHWH will choose in one of your tribes, there you shall offer your burnt offerings, and there you shall do all that I am commanding you.

12:23 Only be sure that you do not eat the blood, for the blood is the life, and you shall not eat the life with the flesh.

12:28 Be careful to obey all these words that I command you, that it may go well with you and with your children after you forever, when you do what is good and right in the sight of YHWH your Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutg and 4QDeuth preserve portions of Chapter 12.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 12 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 12:5 — the place that YHWH your Creator will choose to put his name. The centralization of worship at the single chosen place is the defining architectural principle of Devarim's legislation. The chosen place is not named in Devarim — the name Yerushalayim does not appear in the Torah. The legislation is prospective: YHWH will choose. The Temple Mount's designation as the chosen place is confirmed through the historical books — Shemu'el Bet, Melakhim Aleph. Devarim creates the legal framework; history fills in the location. The principle — one people, one covenant, one place of central worship — is the anchor of the Devarim law code.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 13 The Test of the Prophet — The Enticer to Other Deities — The Apostate City

13:1 If a prophet or a dreamer of dreams arises among you and gives you a sign or a wonder,

13:2 And the sign or wonder that he tells you comes to pass, and if he says: Let us go after other deities, which you have not known, and let us serve them,

13:3 You shall not listen to the words of that prophet or that dreamer of dreams. For YHWH your Creator is testing you, to know whether you love YHWH your Creator with all your heart and with all your soul.

13:4 You shall walk after YHWH your Creator and fear him and keep his commandments and obey his voice, and you shall serve him and hold fast to him.

13:5 But that prophet or that dreamer of dreams shall be put to death, because he has taught rebellion against YHWH your Creator, who brought you out of the land of Egypt and redeemed you out of the house of slavery, to make you leave the way in which YHWH your Creator commanded you to walk. So you shall purge the evil from your midst.

13:6 If your brother, the son of your mother, or your son or your daughter or the wife you embrace or your friend who is as your own soul entices you secretly, saying: Let us go and serve other deities, which neither you nor your fathers have known —

13:8 You shall not yield to him or listen to him, nor shall your eye pity him, nor shall you spare him, nor shall you conceal him.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeuth and 4QDeuti preserve portions of Chapter 13.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 13 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13:1-3 — if a prophet gives you a sign or wonder and it comes to pass, but says: let us go after other deities — you shall not listen. The test of a true prophet in Devarim is not predictive accuracy alone. A prophet can perform signs and wonders that come to pass and still be a false prophet if the content of his message leads away from YHWH toward other deities. The criterion of true prophecy is theological content, not miraculous confirmation. YHWH himself may allow a false sign to test whether the people love him more than they love the miraculous. This principle is one of the most significant anti-deception safeguards in the entire Torah.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 14 The Dietary Laws Restated — The Tithe Laws

14:1 You are the sons of YHWH your Creator. You shall not cut yourselves or make any baldness on your foreheads for the dead.

14:2 For you are a people holy to YHWH your Creator, and YHWH has chosen you to be a people for his treasured possession, out of all the peoples who are on the face of the earth.

14:3 You shall not eat any abomination.

14:4 These are the animals you may eat: the ox, the sheep, the goat,

14:6 And every animal that parts the hoof and has the hoof cloven in two and chews the cud, among the animals, you may eat.

14:9 Of all that are in the waters you may eat these: whatever has fins and scales you may eat.

14:21 You shall not eat anything that has died naturally. You may give it to the sojourner who is within your towns, that he may eat it, or you may sell it to a foreigner. For you are a people holy to YHWH your Creator.

14:22 You shall tithe all the yield of your seed that comes from the field year by year.

14:28 At the end of every three years you shall bring out all the tithe of your produce in the same year and lay it up within your towns.

14:29 And the Levite, because he has no portion or inheritance with you, and the sojourner, the fatherless, and the widow, who are within your towns, shall come and eat and be filled, that YHWH your Creator may bless you in all the work of your hands that you do.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeuti and 4QDeutj preserve portions of Chapter 14.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 14 in full. The dietary laws of Chapter 14 parallel Vayikra 11 with some variations in ordering. The DSS preserves both versions without harmonizing them.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14:1 — you are the sons of YHWH your Creator. This is one of the most direct statements of the covenant people's relationship to YHWH in the Torah. Sons — banim. Not servants, not subjects, not clients — sons. The prohibition that follows — do not cut yourselves for the dead — is grounded in this filial status. The mourning practices of the surrounding nations involve bodily mutilation. YHWH's sons do not disfigure the body YHWH made in his image as an expression of grief. The covenant identity shapes even the expression of sorrow.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 15 The Shemitah Release — The Release of Servants — The Firstborn

15:1 At the end of every seven years you shall grant a release.

15:2 And this is the manner of the release: every creditor shall release what he has lent to his neighbor. He shall not exact it of his neighbor, his brother, because YHWH's release has been proclaimed.

15:4 But there will be no poor among you — for YHWH will bless you in the land that YHWH your Creator is giving you for an inheritance to possess —

15:7 If among you, one of your brothers should become poor, in any of your towns within your land that YHWH your Creator is giving you, you shall not harden your heart or shut your hand against your poor brother,

15:8 But you shall open wide your hand to him and lend him sufficient for his need, whatever it may be.

15:9 Take care lest there be an unworthy thought in your heart and you say: The seventh year, the year of release is near, and your eye look grudgingly on your poor brother, and you give him nothing, and he cry to YHWH against you, and you be guilty of sin.

15:10 You shall give to him freely, and your heart shall not be grudging when you give to him, because for this YHWH your Creator will bless you in all your work and in all that you undertake.

15:11 For there will never cease to be poor in the land. Therefore I command you: You shall open wide your hand to your brother, to the needy and to the poor, in your land.

15:15 You shall remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt, and YHWH your Creator redeemed you — therefore I command you this today.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutj and 4QDeutk1 preserve portions of Chapter 15.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 15 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 15:11 — there will never cease to be poor in the land. Therefore I command you: you shall open wide your hand. The existence of the poor is not presented as a problem to be solved by the legislation but as a permanent reality that generates a permanent obligation. The Torah does not promise the elimination of poverty through economic legislation alone. It commands the open hand as the constant covenant response to the poor who will always be present. The covenant economics of Devarim — the shemitah release, the open hand, the third-year tithe — do not claim to produce a poverty-free society. They claim to produce a society with a covenant obligation to the poor that never expires.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 16 The Three Pilgrimage Feasts — Pesach, Shavuot, Sukkot

16:1 Observe the month of Aviv and keep the Pesach to YHWH your Creator, for in the month of Aviv YHWH your Creator brought you out of Egypt by night.

16:2 And you shall offer the Pesach sacrifice to YHWH your Creator, from the flock or the herd, at the place that YHWH will choose, to make his name dwell there.

16:3 You shall eat no leavened bread with it. Seven days you shall eat it with unleavened bread, the bread of affliction — for you came out of the land of Egypt in haste — that all the days of your life you may remember the day when you came out of the land of Egypt.

16:9 You shall count seven weeks. Begin to count the seven weeks from the time the sickle is first put to the standing grain.

16:10 Then you shall keep the Feast of Weeks to YHWH your Creator with the tribute of a freewill offering from your hand, which you shall give as YHWH your Creator blesses you.

16:11 And you shall rejoice before YHWH your Creator, you and your son and your daughter, your male servant and your female servant, the Levite who is within your towns, the sojourner, the fatherless, and the widow who are among you, at the place that YHWH your Creator will choose, to make his name dwell there.

16:13 You shall keep the Feast of Booths seven days, when you have gathered in the produce from your threshing floor and your winepress.

16:14 You shall rejoice in your feast, you and your son and your daughter, your male servant and your female servant, the Levite, the sojourner, the fatherless, and the widow who are within your towns.

16:16 Three times a year all your males shall appear before YHWH your Creator at the place that he will choose: at the Feast of Unleavened Bread, at the Feast of Weeks, and at the Feast of Booths. They shall not appear before YHWH empty-handed.

16:17 Every man shall give as he is able, according to the blessing of YHWH your Creator that he has given you.

16:18 You shall appoint judges and officers in all your towns that YHWH your Creator is giving you, according to your tribes, and they shall judge the people with righteous judgment.

16:19 You shall not pervert justice. You shall not show partiality, and you shall not accept a bribe, for a bribe blinds the eyes of the wise and subverts the cause of the righteous.

16:20 Justice, and only justice, you shall follow, that you may live and inherit the land that YHWH your Creator is giving you.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutk1 and 4QDeutk2 preserve portions of Chapter 16.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 16 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 16:14 — you shall rejoice in your feast, you and your son and your daughter, your male servant and your female servant, the Levite, the sojourner, the fatherless, and the widow. The feast invitation in Devarim is consistently expanded beyond the household. All three pilgrimage feasts include the Levite, the sojourner, the fatherless, and the widow in the celebration. The covenant community's joy is not a private family matter. The appointed feast is a community event that specifically draws in those who have no land inheritance — Levites — and those who are economically vulnerable — sojourners, orphans, widows. The feast calendar is also a social equity calendar.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 16:20 — justice, and only justice, you shall follow. Tzedek tzedek tirdof — literally: justice, justice, you shall pursue. The doubling of the word justice in the Hebrew is the Torah's most emphatic single-word emphasis. Not approximate justice. Not justice when convenient. Justice pursued, hunted down, followed relentlessly. The covenant governance of Yisra'EL rests on this principle as its foundation. Verse 16:18 places the appointment of judges before the feast calendar legislation closes — covenant justice is the civic expression of the same covenant that governs worship.

DEVARIM — CHAPTER 17 The Judiciary — The King — The Limits of Royal Power

17:2 If there is found among you, within any of your towns that YHWH your Creator is giving you, a man or woman who does what is evil in the sight of YHWH your Creator, in transgressing his covenant,

17:6 On the evidence of two witnesses or of three witnesses the one who is to die shall be put to death — a person shall not be put to death on the evidence of one witness.

17:8 If any case arises requiring decision between one kind of homicide and another, one kind of legal right and another, or one kind of assault and another, any case within your towns that is too difficult for you, then you shall arise and go up to the place that YHWH your Creator will choose.

17:10 Then you shall do according to what they declare to you from that place that YHWH will choose. And you shall be careful to do according to all that they direct you.

17:14 When you come to the land that YHWH your Creator is giving you, and you possess it and dwell in it and then say: I will set a king over me, like all the nations that are around me,

17:15 You may indeed set a king over you whom YHWH your Creator will choose. One from among your brothers you shall set as king over you. You may not put a foreigner over you, who is not your brother.

17:16 Only he must not acquire many horses for himself or cause the people to return to Egypt in order to acquire many horses, since YHWH has said to you: You shall never return that way again.

17:17 And he shall not acquire many wives for himself, lest his heart turn away, nor shall he acquire for himself excessive silver and gold.

17:18 And when he sits on the throne of his kingdom, he shall write for himself in a book a copy of this law, approved by the Levitical priests.

17:19 And it shall be with him, and he shall read in it all the days of his life, that he may learn to fear YHWH his Creator by keeping all the words of this law and these statutes, and doing them.

17:20 That his heart may not be lifted up above his brothers, and that he may not turn aside from the commandment, either to the right hand or to the left, so that he may continue long in his kingdom, he and his children, in Yisra'EL.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QDeutl and 4QDeutm preserve portions of Chapter 17. The law of the king in verses 17:14-20 is one of the most significant political texts in the Qumran Devarim manuscripts. The Temple Scroll — 11QTemple — from Cave 11 contains an extensively expanded version of this legislation.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 17 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verses 17:16-17 — the king must not acquire many horses, must not return to Egypt for horses, must not acquire many wives, must not acquire excessive silver and gold. The Devarim king law is the most explicit constitutional limitation on royal power in the ancient world. The king is not above the law — he is required to write out the Torah with his own hand and read it all the days of his life. He is explicitly prohibited from the three instruments of imperial power: military capacity (horses), political alliance (wives), and wealth accumulation (silver and gold). Melakhim Aleph documents how Shlomo violated all three — horses from Egypt, seven hundred wives, accumulated wealth beyond measure. The Torah's king law is a direct indictment of imperial kingship written into the covenant before Yisra'EL had a king.

END OF DEVARIM PART ONE — VOLUME FIVE

Chapters 1 through 17 — The First and Second Addresses — The Shema — The Covenant Law

𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

CONTINUES IN DEVARIM PART TWO

Chapters 18 through 34 — The Prophets — The Curses — The Song of Moshe — The Death of Moshe
